preamble: This fascinating phonetic transformation illustrates a significant sound change in the history of the Italic languages, including Latin. It describes how the Proto-Indo-European voiced aspirated alveolar plosive, represented as `*dʰ`, evolved into the voiceless labiodental fricative `f` in word-initial positions within Proto-Italic. This shift is a hallmark feature distinguishing the Italic branch from other Indo-European language families.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing (voiced to voiceless), Fricativization (plosive to fricative), Place Shift (alveolar to labiodental), Aspiration Loss

examples.note: Meaning 'son'. This is a direct descendant from Proto-Indo-European *dʰeh₁-yos to Latin fīlius.
Meaning 'to make, do'. Derived from the Proto-Indo-European root *dʰeh₁-.

sources: Origin of the verb crēdere : r/latin - Reddit
